text.title: Issue Prioritization and CI Integration
text.summary: Priority rubric for a11y issues, CI integration patterns, false-positive management, and the agentic testing pipeline.

rule.statement: Assign every issue one of four priority levels based on whether it blocks access completely, creates a significant barrier, creates difficulty, or is an enhancement.
rule.rationale: Without explicit triage, all issues compete equally and critical ones are delayed. Priority drives fix timeline, not just backlog ordering.

example.code: Priority | Definition | Fix Target Critical | Blocks access completely (no workaround) | Immediately / hotfix High | Major barrier; workaround is difficult | Current sprint Medium | Creates difficulty; workaround available | Next sprint Low | Enhancement; not blocking any task | Backlog

section.title: CI Integration

rule.statement: Run axe + Pa11y on every PR against a known URL; snapshot axe JSON per release and diff against the previous release to detect regressions.
rule.rationale: Without a CI gate, a11y regressions accumulate silently between releases. Diffing JSON identifies whether a new release introduced new violations.

section.reference: Shell script running axe + Pa11y + Lighthouse with JSON output and summary.md

list.item: Tag Playwright/Cypress tests with @a11y-critical for PR CI; run full crawl nightly only.
For SPA: wait for networkidle AND a route-specific selector before scanning to avoid pre-render snapshots.
Maintain an a11y-exemptions.yml with WCAG criterion + justification + expiry date — never silently ignore violations.
Dismiss cookie banners/consent overlays before measurement; log which cookie state was used.

antipattern.description: Accepting "0 axe violations" as compliance without checking the "incomplete" array.
antipattern.reason: Items in the axe "incomplete" array are criteria the scanner could not evaluate (not "passed"). Treating incomplete as pass creates systematic blind spots.

antipattern.description: Running agent "fix until axe passes" loops more than 3 iterations without an AT regression test.
antipattern.reason: Automated fix loops produce ARIA soup that passes axe but breaks real screen readers. Limit auto-fix iterations and require a human screen reader check after each batch of fixes.

section.title: Agentic Pipeline
section.p: Step 1 (automated) and Step 2 (keyboard via Playwright) and Step 5 (triage and doc generation) are agent-driveable. Steps 3 (screen reader) and 4 (cognitive) require human-in-loop — agent prepares scripts and templates, a tester executes. Pipeline: scanner agent → keyboard-bot → triage agent → report writer.

list.item: Scanner agent: run axe + Pa11y, parse JSON, group violations by WCAG criterion with fix recommendation.
Triage agent: deduplicate by selector+rule across tools, map to WCAG 2.2 AA, assign priority per rubric.
Report writer: populate audit-report.md template from triage output; flag untested criteria as "Not Tested", never "Supports".
